verses.text: Job answers with biting irony: how greatly they have 'helped' the powerless and 'counselled' the unwise — whose spirit spoke through them? Their wisdom is borrowed and useless. Hollow words bring no help to a soul in real distress.

Then Job soars: the dead tremble below, hell is naked before God, and 'he hangeth the earth upon nothing.' He sees God's reach over all realms. The Maker who suspends the world on emptiness is worthy of unbounded awe.

Then Job soars: the dead tremble below, hell is naked before God, and 'he hangeth the earth upon nothing.' He sees God's reach over all realms. The Maker who suspends the world on emptiness is worthy of unbounded awe.

God binds the waters in thick clouds that do not burst, veils His throne, and sets a bound around the sea where light meets darkness. He governs sky and ocean with ease. Every cloud and shoreline is held by His ordering hand.

God binds the waters in thick clouds that do not burst, veils His throne, and sets a bound around the sea where light meets darkness. He governs sky and ocean with ease. Every cloud and shoreline is held by His ordering hand.

The pillars of heaven tremble at His rebuke; He stills the sea, and 'by his spirit he hath garnished the heavens.' Creation bows to His power. The same hand that calms the storm adorned the starry sky.

The pillars of heaven tremble at His rebuke; He stills the sea, and 'by his spirit he hath garnished the heavens.' Creation bows to His power. The same hand that calms the storm adorned the starry sky.

The pillars of heaven tremble at His rebuke; He stills the sea, and 'by his spirit he hath garnished the heavens.' Creation bows to His power. The same hand that calms the storm adorned the starry sky.

Yet these, Job says, are but the outskirts of His ways — a faint whisper; 'the thunder of his power who can understand?' All we know of God is the merest edge. His full greatness lies forever beyond us, and we adore what we cannot comprehend.
